LAW AND MOTION 302, JUL-13-2023. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION. RULING - ARGUED. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'S (NISSAN) MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Jul-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Hearing RE: Defendant NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC.'s Motion to Compel Arbitration and Stay Action.

Appearances (Zoom):

Allen Amarkarian, Esq., QUILL & ARROW, LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

JAD I. DOUDAR, Esq., LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415) 362-2580, for Defendant.

Ruling - ARGUED. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Nissan North America, Inc.'s (Nissan) motion to compel arbitration is denied. The Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) makes agreements to arbitrate "valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract." (AT&T Mobility LLC v. Concepcion (2011) 563 U.S. 333, 336; 9 U.S.C. sec. 2.) Plaintiff Honorio Altun entered into a sales contract with a dealership. (Compl. 8.) The manufacturer of the car, Nissan, is not a party to this sales contract. Nissan seeks to compel arbitration against Altun through equitable estoppel. Under equitable estoppel, a nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause against a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate claims against the nonsignatory which are "intimately founded in and intertwined with the underlying contractual obligations." (Ochoa v. Ford Motor Company (2023) 89 Cal.App.5th 1324, 1332-33.) The complaint alleges that "[t]hese causes of action arise out of warranty and repair obligations of NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. in connection with a vehicle Plaintiff purchased and for which NISSAN NORTH AMERICA, INC. issued a written warranty." (Compl. 4.) Nissan argues that Altun's claims are therefore intimately founded in and intertwined with the sales contract. (Nissan P&A at 12:04-12:08.) Not so. Altun's claims invoke obligations imposed against Nissan by statute, rather than by the sales contract. Nissan contends that the claims are founded in the sales contract because Altun's right to assert the warranties only came into existence when he purchased the car by signing the sales contract. However, this argument confuses the concept of 'claims founded in and intertwined with the agreement' with 'but-for causation.' (See Montemayor v. Ford Motor Company (Issued June 26, 2023) WL 4181909 at *7.) While the claims would not arise but-for the sales contract, they are not founded in and intertwined with the sales contract.
Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
